originContent: A tool to download purchased e-books from different publishers.
translatedContent: A tool to download purchased e-books from different publishers.

originContent: | Publisher | Website | Best Quality | Selectable Text | Hyperrefs | Notes |
translatedContent: | Publisher | Website | Best Quality | Selectable Text | Hyperrefs | Notes |

originContent: | Cornelsen ("Old Method") | cornelsen.de | Image (8617px x 11792px) composition | ✓ | ✗ |  |
translatedContent: | Cornelsen ("Old Method") | cornelsen.de | Image (8617px x 11792px) composition | ✓ | ✗ |  |

originContent: | Helbling Media App | helbling.com | Lossless PDF | ✓ | ✗ | |
translatedContent: | Helbling Media App | helbling.com | Lossless PDF | ✓ | ✗ | |

originContent: [1]: Website allows to download files that are not owned
translatedContent: [1]: Website allows to download files that are not owned

originContent: [2]: No Account needed, only the book id
translatedContent: [2]: No Account needed, only the book id

originContent: At first, clone or download the repository.
translatedContent: At first, clone or download the repository.

originContent: The tool uses image processing libraries and has to be executed with nodejs, therefore some dependencies are required.
translatedContent: The tool uses image processing libraries and has to be executed with nodejs, therefore some dependencies are required.

originContent: You can eiher use the installation scripts (`init.bat` for Windows, `init.sh` for Debian based distros) or use manual installation. The installation scripts will download all dependencies for you. To execute them, double click the `.bat` file on windows or execute the `.sh` file in the terminal on linux after adding execution permissions to the `.sh` file.
translatedContent: You can eiher use the installation scripts (`init.bat` for Windows, `init.sh` for Debian based distros) or use manual installation. The installation scripts will download all dependencies for you. To execute them, double click the `.bat` file on windows or execute the `.sh` file in the terminal on linux after adding execution permissions to the `.sh` file.

originContent: At first, clone or download the repository. Then follow the os-specific instructions below:
translatedContent: At first, clone or download the repository. Then follow the os-specific instructions below:

originContent: 1. Download `unifont-15.0.01.ttf` from http://www.unifoundry.com/pub/unifont/unifont-15.0.01/font-builds/unifont-15.0.01.ttf and put it in the project root
translatedContent: 1. Download `unifont-15.0.01.ttf` from http://www.unifoundry.com/pub/unifont/unifont-15.0.01/font-builds/unifont-15.0.01.ttf and put it in the project root

originContent: 1. Install `ffmpeg` and put the ffmpeg executable (named as `ffmpeg`) in the project root or add it to your path environment variable, if you need help you can follow [this](https://phoenixnap.com/kb/ffmpeg-windows) tutorial
translatedContent: 1. Install `ffmpeg` and put the ffmpeg executable (named as `ffmpeg`) in the project root or add it to your path environment variable, if you need help you can follow [this](https://phoenixnap.com/kb/ffmpeg-windows) tutorial

originContent: 1. Install `mutool` and put the mutool executable (named as `mutool`) in the project root or add it to your path environment variable, you can find the most recent builds [here](https://mupdf.com/releases). Search for releases with `windows`. (only required for book2look and cornelsen.ch)
translatedContent: 1. Install `mutool` and put the mutool executable (named as `mutool`) in the project root or add it to your path environment variable, you can find the most recent builds [here](https://mupdf.com/releases). Search for releases with `windows`. (only required for book2look and cornelsen.ch)

originContent: 1. Install `nodejs` and `npm` if you haven't already, you can find help [here](https://phoenixnap.com/kb/install-node-js-npm-on-windows)
translatedContent: 1. Install `nodejs` and `npm` if you haven't already, you can find help [here](https://phoenixnap.com/kb/install-node-js-npm-on-windows)

originContent: 1. Optionally install `image magick` and put the magick executable (named as `magick`) in the project root or add it to your path environment variable (required only for cornelsen "old method")
translatedContent: 1. Optionally install `image magick` and put the magick executable (named as `magick`) in the project root or add it to your path environment variable (required only for cornelsen "old method")

originContent: 1. Install packages `ffmpeg`, `nodejs`, `mutool`/`mupdf-tools`/`mupdf`  (required for book2look and cornelsen.ch) and optionally `imagemagick` (required only for cornelsen "old method") with your favorite package manager
translatedContent: 1. Install packages `ffmpeg`, `nodejs`, `mutool`/`mupdf-tools`/`mupdf`  (required for book2look and cornelsen.ch) and optionally `imagemagick` (required only for cornelsen "old method") with your favorite package manager

originContent: - If you have nix-direnv, run `direnv allow` to obtain all dependencies
translatedContent: - If you have nix-direnv, run `direnv allow` to obtain all dependencies

originContent: Complete the setup by running `npm install` in the project directory and
translatedContent: Complete the setup by running `npm install` in the project directory and

originContent: start the program by executing `npm start` from the project directory.
translatedContent: start the program by executing `npm start` from the project directory.

originContent: ## Saving credentials to avoid entering them every time (optional)
translatedContent: ## Saving credentials to avoid entering them every time (optional)

originContent: You can save your credentials in a `config.json` file to avoid entering them every time you start the program. To do this, create a file named `config.json` in the project root and add the following content:
translatedContent: You can save your credentials in a `config.json` file to avoid entering them every time you start the program. To do this, create a file named `config.json` in the project root and add the following content:

originContent: Replace all the square brackets with your data. Publisher keys are: `cornelsen`, `klett`, `allango`, `scook`, `westermann`, `clicknstudy`, `clicknteach`, `kiosquemag`, `cornelsench`, `book2look`, `helbling`
translatedContent: Replace all the square brackets with your data. Publisher keys are: `cornelsen`, `klett`, `allango`, `scook`, `westermann`, `clicknstudy`, `clicknteach`, `kiosquemag`, `cornelsench`, `book2look`, `helbling`

originContent: Cornelsen, Click-n-Study, Klett, and Westermann have token-login support, allowing login using Bildungslogin accounts.
translatedContent: Cornelsen, Click-n-Study, Klett, and Westermann have token-login support, allowing login using Bildungslogin accounts.

originContent: 1. Go into Storage devtools (SHIFT+F9, or alternatively press CTRL+SHIFT+I and click on Storage tab).
translatedContent: 1. Go into Storage devtools (SHIFT+F9, or alternatively press CTRL+SHIFT+I and click on Storage tab).

originContent: 1. Select Local storage in the devtools (possibly also need to select the website in it)
translatedContent: 1. Select Local storage in the devtools (possibly also need to select the website in it)

originContent: 1. Scroll down in the table that opens until you find `id_token`. It should be a long alphanumeric string.
translatedContent: 1. Scroll down in the table that opens until you find `id_token`. It should be a long alphanumeric string.

originContent: 1. Use it as the password, and use `token` as the username.
translatedContent: 1. Use it as the password, and use `token` as the username.

originContent: 1. Go into Storage devtools (SHIFT+F9, or alternatively press CTRL+SHIFT+I and click on Storage tab).
translatedContent: 1. Go into Storage devtools (SHIFT+F9, or alternatively press CTRL+SHIFT+I and click on Storage tab).

originContent: 1. Select Cookies in the devtools (possibly also need to select the website in it)
translatedContent: 1. Select Cookies in the devtools (possibly also need to select the website in it)

originContent: 1. Scroll down in the table that opens until you find `CASSID`. It should have an alphanumeric string afterwards.
translatedContent: 1. Scroll down in the table that opens until you find `CASSID`. It should have an alphanumeric string afterwards.

originContent: 1. Copy that (the random looking characters). (Double click, then ctrl+c)
translatedContent: 1. Copy that (the random looking characters). (Double click, then ctrl+c)

originContent: 1. Use it as the password, and use `token` as the username.
translatedContent: 1. Use it as the password, and use `token` as the username.

originContent: 1. Go into Storage devtools (SHIFT+F9, or alternatively press CTRL+SHIFT+I and click on Storage tab).
translatedContent: 1. Go into Storage devtools (SHIFT+F9, or alternatively press CTRL+SHIFT+I and click on Storage tab).

originContent: 1. Select Cookies in the devtools (possibly also need to select the website in it)
translatedContent: 1. Select Cookies in the devtools (possibly also need to select the website in it)

originContent: 1. Scroll down in the table that opens until you find `SESSION`. It should have an alphanumeric string afterwards.
translatedContent: 1. Scroll down in the table that opens until you find `SESSION`. It should have an alphanumeric string afterwards.

originContent: 1. Copy that (the random looking characters). (Double click, then ctrl+c)
translatedContent: 1. Copy that (the random looking characters). (Double click, then ctrl+c)

originContent: 1. Use it as the password, and use **the URL of the book viewer you are on** as the username.
translatedContent: 1. Use it as the password, and use **the URL of the book viewer you are on** as the username.

originContent: 1. Go into Networking devtools (CTRL+SHIFT+E, or alternatively press CTRL+SHIFT+I and click on Network tab).
translatedContent: 1. Go into Networking devtools (CTRL+SHIFT+E, or alternatively press CTRL+SHIFT+I and click on Network tab).

originContent: 1. Tick `Persist logs` (possibly in Gear menu, likely at top right somewhere)
translatedContent: 1. Tick `Persist logs` (possibly in Gear menu, likely at top right somewhere)

originContent: 1. Enter `/api/user` into the "Filter URLs" field of devtools
translatedContent: 1. Enter `/api/user` into the "Filter URLs" field of devtools

originContent: 1. Click the first entry that does *not* say OPTIONS
translatedContent: 1. Click the first entry that does *not* say OPTIONS

originContent: 1. A little pane to the side should open. Click on its Headers tab
translatedContent: 1. A little pane to the side should open. Click on its Headers tab

originContent: 1. Enter `auth` into its "Filter headers" field (if available)
translatedContent: 1. Enter `auth` into its "Filter headers" field (if available)

originContent: 1. Scroll down until you find a long string of letters and numbers
translatedContent: 1. Scroll down until you find a long string of letters and numbers

originContent: 1. Copy it and paste it into a text document
translatedContent: 1. Copy it and paste it into a text document

originContent: 1. Copy it from there again, but make sure NOT to select the "Authorization: Bearer " part, only the long string at the end.
translatedContent: 1. Copy it from there again, but make sure NOT to select the "Authorization: Bearer " part, only the long string at the end.

originContent: 1. Use it as the password, and use `token` as the username.
translatedContent: 1. Use it as the password, and use `token` as the username.

originContent: Feel free to create issues and pull requests to contribute to the project.
translatedContent: Feel free to create issues and pull requests to contribute to the project.

originContent: This project is for educational purposes only. The project is not responsible for any misuse of the software. Depending on your jurisdiction, it may be illegal to use this software to download e-books without the consent of the publisher. In other jurisdictions, it may be legal to download e-books for personal use only. Please check your local laws before using this software.
translatedContent: This project is for educational purposes only. The project is not responsible for any misuse of the software. Depending on your jurisdiction, it may be illegal to use this software to download e-books without the consent of the publisher. In other jurisdictions, it may be legal to download e-books for personal use only. Please check your local laws before using this software.
